states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial state upon connection before authentication begins.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User has provided a username; waiting for password.

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters have been established via passive or active mode negotiation.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: An active file or directory listing transfer is occurring.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: A rename operation is mid-way; source path accepted.

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, and APPE commands require a successful login (USER followed by PASS).
A data connection must be established via PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT before executing LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, or APPE.
The QUIT command can be sent at any point during the session to terminate.
If a PASS command results in a 530 response, the client may attempt to authenticate again by sending a new USER command.
The RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
Sending PASS without first sending USER will result in a 530 Not Logged In error.
USER.username: Must follow a valid USER command name string.
PASS.password: Must follow a valid PASS command password string.
TYPE.type-code: Must be A (ASCII) or I (Image/Binary).
PORT.address-info: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (host and port components).
CWD.path: Must be a valid path string for the server file system.
XCWD.path: Must be a valid path string for the server file system.
RETR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
STOR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
APPE.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
DELE.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
RNFR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
RNTO.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: The USER command initiates the login sequence, moving from the initial connection state to waiting for password.
A successful PASS command (response 230) completes the login.
A failed PASS (response 530) keeps the user in the login phase for retries.
PWD is a metadata command that does not change the authentication state.
Navigation commands do not change the authentication state.
PORT specifies the address for the data channel.
Triggers a data transfer, though often preceded by PORT/PASV. In some traces, it may appear direct if defaults are used or if modeled as such.
A QUIT command can terminate the session during active transfers.
Standard logout transition.
STAT is an informational command.
ACCT provides account info, usually staying within the authenticated context.
Navigation command.
Navigation command.
Metadata command.
Navigation command.
File system modification command.
File system modification command.
File system modification command.
File system modification command.
File system modification command.
Configuration command.
Configuration command.
Mount command, remains in authenticated state.
Capability discovery command.
Informational command.
No-operation command.
Metadata command.
Metadata command.
IPv6/Extended Passive mode negotiation.
IPv6/Extended Active mode negotiation.
Directory listing transfer.
File append transfer.
Transfer command using prepared channel.
Transfer command using prepared channel.
Transfer command using prepared channel.
Transfer command using prepared channel.
Transfer command using prepared channel.
Returns to control state after transfer completion. Labeled 'via LIST' to indicate the initiating command's completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
QUIT can terminate the connection even before login.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP authentication sequence defined in RFC 959.

status_changes.description: If a PASS command results in a 530 response, the client may attempt to authenticate again by sending a new USER command.
status_changes.rationale: A 530 error indicates a login failure, typically allowing for a restart of the sequence.

status_changes.description: The RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: Rename sequence requires source (RNFR) then destination (RNTO) per RFC 959.

status_changes.description: Sending PASS without first sending USER will result in a 530 Not Logged In error.
status_changes.rationale: The server requires the username to verify the password.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Must be a valid path string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: CWD requires a directory path as an argument.

status_changes.description: XCWD.path: Must be a valid path string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: XCWD is an experimental version of CWD with the same argument requirements.

status_changes.description: RETR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: RETR requires the path of the file to be retrieved.

status_changes.description: STOR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: STOR requires the path of the file to be stored.

status_changes.description: APPE.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: APPE requires the path of the file to append to.

status_changes.description: DELE.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: DELE requires the path of the file to be deleted.

status_changes.description: RNFR.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: RNFR requires the path of the source file to rename.

status_changes.description: RNTO.filename: Must be a valid filename string for the server file system.
status_changes.rationale: RNTO requires the path of the destination filename.
